message:
feat(memo): garbage-collect inspected quarantine tombstones and rehydrate on reinsert

Adds `ContentAddressedMemoCache::garbage_collect_quarantined(key)`,
which removes a quarantined tombstone and returns its
`MemoQuarantineInspectionItem` (carrying the reason string and
the original `MemoKey`). This closes the loop that
`quarantine_inspection_items()` opened — previously, an operator
could inspect the quarantined entries but had no in-memory way to
retire them after investigation, so the tombstone would stay
resident forever.

Semantics (unit-tested in
`garbage_collect_quarantined_entry_after_inspection`):

  - Returns `Some(item)` when the key was present in the
    quarantine map, or `None` otherwise.
  - After a successful collection, `quarantine_summary().quarantined_entries`
    drops by one and a fresh `get(key)` returns `MemoLookup::Miss`
    (not a sticky quarantine hit), so subsequent lookups behave
    exactly like the key was never seen.
  - A subsequent `insert(key, new_value)` succeeds and yields a
    regular cache Hit — so rehabilitated content can be brought
    back into the cache without an explicit `quarantine` reset
    call. This is the intended recovery path for "the operator
    checked the quarantine reason, patched the upstream generator,
    and now wants the replay result cached again."

Doc comment is explicit that this is in-memory metadata GC only —
persistent artifact retention (e.g. reclaimable-bytes accounting
in the lexical generation cleanup plan) is a separate concern and
still owned by the caller.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
